reasoning: The summary maintains a neutral tone and avoids adopting the source’s few loaded framings (e.g., it omits Reuters’ “anti-vaccine conspiracy theorist” label and softens “desperate” to “seeking”). Verbs are mostly factual, and claims are attributed to polls or groups. Minor issues include use of the term “spoilers” and slight emphasis on Democratic concerns, plus omission of some perspectives (e.g., Trump campaign boast, Tony Lyons’ critique, West/Stein mentions). Overall, it is more neutral than the source.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (seeking, could capture, raised) with only mild “looms”/“spoilers.”
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Key claims are attributed to Gallup/Reuters-Ipsos or sourced fundraising figures.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative ideas (spoiler risk, voter desire) are attributed to parties/polls.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids charged language; softens source’s stronger wording.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about internal motives without attribution.
custom_reasoning.slant: Notes concerns of both parties; slight emphasis on Democratic worry but balanced overall.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits some perspectives present (Trump campaign boast, Lyons’ anti–two-party critique, West/Stein).
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Minimal subjective phrasing (“significant opportunities”) without strong loading.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically neutral vocabulary; “spoilers” is standard electoral term.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced presentation of risks and interest in third-party bids.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats polls, campaigns, and groups evenly without privileging one.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than source; avoids loaded descriptors like “conspiracy theorist.”
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Covers major threads (polling, RFK Jr., No Labels, two-party concerns) but skips several actor perspectives mentioned in the article.
